Generated Narrative: StructureDefinition provider-parameters-multi-member-match-bundle-in

NameFlagsCard.TypeDescription & Constraints
Parameters 0..*ParametersOperation Request or Response
Slices for parameter 1..*BackboneElementProvider member match request parameters
Slice: Unordered, Open by value:name
parameter:MembersToMatch 1..*BackboneElementOperation Parameter
name 1..1stringMember elements to match for provider access
Required Pattern: MembersToMatch
Slices for part 3..*Components of provider member match request
Slice: Unordered, Open by value:name
part:MemberPatient 1..1BackboneElementNamed part of a multi-part parameter
name 1..1stringCurrent member demographics
Required Pattern: MemberPatient
resource 1..1HRexPatientDemographics(1.1.0)Patient demographic details
part:CoverageToMatch 1..1BackboneElementNamed part of a multi-part parameter
name 1..1stringCurrent member coverage
Required Pattern: CoverageToMatch
resource 1..1HRexCoverage(1.1.0)Current coverage details
part:TreatmentAttestation 1..1BackboneElementNamed part of a multi-part parameter
name 1..1stringProvider treatment attestation
Required Pattern: TreatmentAttestation
resource 1..1ProviderTreatmentAttestationProvider attestation to treatment relationship
part:CoverageToLink 0..1BackboneElementNamed part of a multi-part parameter
name 1..1stringOptional coverage to link (typically not used)
Required Pattern: CoverageToLink
resource 0..1HRexCoverage(1.1.0)Insurance or medical plan or a payment agreement

Documentation for this format

name: ProviderMultiMemberMatchRequestParameters
title: Provider $multi-member-match Request
description: A Parameters profile defining the inputs to a $bulk-member-match operation performed by a payer system in response to a provider request. This differs from the payer-to-payer multi-member-match by including current member demographics, current coverage, and provider treatment attestation rather than patient consent for payer-to-payer data exchange.
copyright: Used by permission of HL7 International, all rights reserved Creative Commons License

element.short: Operation Request or Response
element.definition: This resource is a non-persisted resource used to pass information into and back from an [operation](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/operations.html). It has no other use, and there is no RESTful endpoint associated with it.
element.comment: The parameters that may be used are defined by the OperationDefinition resource.

element.short: Logical id of this artifact
element.definition: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.
element.comment: The only time that a resource does not have an id is when it is being submitted to the server using a create operation.

element.short: Metadata about the resource
element.definition: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: A set of rules under which this content was created
element.definition: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.
element.comment: Asserting this rule set restricts the content to be only understood by a limited set of trading partners. This inherently limits the usefulness of the data in the long term. However, the existing health eco-system is highly fractured, and not yet ready to define, collect, and exchange data in a generally computable sense. Wherever possible, implementers and/or specification writers should avoid using this element. Often, when used, the URL is a reference to an implementation guide that defines these special rules as part of it's narrative along with other profiles, value sets, etc.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.isModifierReason: This element is labeled as a modifier because the implicit rules may provide additional knowledge about the resource that modifies it's meaning or interpretation

element.short: Language of the resource content
element.definition: The base language in which the resource is written.
element.comment: Language is provided to support indexing and accessibility (typically, services such as text to speech use the language tag). The html language tag in the narrative applies  to the narrative. The language tag on the resource may be used to specify the language of other presentations generated from the data in the resource. Not all the content has to be in the base language. The Resource.language should not be assumed to apply to the narrative automatically. If a language is specified, it should it also be specified on the div element in the html (see rules in HTML5 for information about the relationship between xml:lang and the html lang attribute).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Provider member match request parameters
element.definition: Parameters for a provider-initiated bulk member match request containing current member demographics, current coverage, and provider treatment attestation.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
A parameter must have one and only one of (value, resource, part)
constraint.expression: (part.exists() and value.empty() and resource.empty()) or (part.empty() and (value.exists() xor resource.exists()))
constraint.xpath: (exists(f:resource) or exists(f:part) or exists(f:*[starts-with(local-name(.), 'value')])) and not(exists(f:*[starts-with(local-name(.), 'value')])) and exists(f:resource))) and not(exists(f:*[starts-with(local-name(.), 'value')])) and exists(f:part))) and not(exists(f:part) and exists(f:resource))

element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

element.definition: The patient's current demographic information including name, date of birth, gender, and identifiers from the provider's system.

element.short: Current member coverage
element.definition: The member's current active coverage with the payer (typically from insurance card or eligibility verification).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: If parameter is a data type
element.definition: If the parameter is a data type.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Current coverage details
element.definition: The member's active coverage information including member ID, subscriber ID, group number, plan details, and payer identifier.
element.comment: The Coverage resource contains the insurance card level information, which is customary to provide on claims and other communications between providers and insurers.

element.short: Provider treatment attestation
element.definition: Provider's attestation of an active treatment relationship with the member. This is NOT patient consent for payer-to-payer data exchange, but rather the provider's certification that they have a treatment relationship justifying access to the member's data.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: If parameter is a data type
element.definition: If the parameter is a data type.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Provider attestation to treatment relationship
element.definition: A Consent resource conforming to the ProviderTreatmentAttestation profile, documenting that the provider has an active treatment relationship with the member and the purposes for which they are requesting access to the member's data.
element.comment: Unlike the payer-to-payer multi-member-match which includes patient consent for data exchange between payers, the provider version includes the provider's attestation to a treatment relationship. This attestation serves as the legal basis for the payer to release data to the provider under HIPAA treatment, payment, and operations (TPO) provisions.

element.short: Optional coverage to link (typically not used)
element.definition: CoverageToLink is typically not used in provider-initiated member match requests as providers are not linking historical coverage to new coverage. This element is only relevant for payer-to-payer member match operations.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: If parameter is a data type
element.definition: If the parameter is a data type.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: Insurance or medical plan or a payment agreement
element.definition: This is the Coverage profile which is used to provide insurance information for scheduling an appointment and/or registering a patient.
element.comment: The Coverage resource contains the insurance card level information, which is customary to provide on claims and other communications between providers and insurers.

element.short: Named part of a multi-part parameter
element.definition: A named part of a multi-part parameter.
element.comment: Only one level of nested parameters is allowed.

element.short: Provider member match request parameters
element.definition: Parameters for a provider-initiated bulk member match request containing current member demographics, current coverage, and provider treatment attestation.

element.short: Member elements to match for provider access
element.definition: A repeating parameter containing current member demographics, current coverage information, and provider treatment attestation for a single member. The name aligns with the ProviderMemberMatch OperationDefinition.

element.short: Components of provider member match request
element.definition: The individual components required for a provider to request access to member data: current patient demographics, current coverage, and provider treatment attestation.

element.short: Current member demographics
element.definition: Current patient demographic information as known by the provider (from provider's EMR or patient-provided information).

element.short: Patient demographic details
element.definition: The patient's current demographic information including name, date of birth, gender, and identifiers from the provider's system.

element.short: Current member coverage
element.definition: The member's current active coverage with the payer (typically from insurance card or eligibility verification).

element.short: Current coverage details
element.definition: The member's active coverage information including member ID, subscriber ID, group number, plan details, and payer identifier.

element.short: Provider treatment attestation
element.definition: Provider's attestation of an active treatment relationship with the member. This is NOT patient consent for payer-to-payer data exchange, but rather the provider's certification that they have a treatment relationship justifying access to the member's data.

element.short: Provider attestation to treatment relationship
element.definition: A Consent resource conforming to the ProviderTreatmentAttestation profile, documenting that the provider has an active treatment relationship with the member and the purposes for which they are requesting access to the member's data.
element.comment: Unlike the payer-to-payer multi-member-match which includes patient consent for data exchange between payers, the provider version includes the provider's attestation to a treatment relationship. This attestation serves as the legal basis for the payer to release data to the provider under HIPAA treatment, payment, and operations (TPO) provisions.

element.short: Optional coverage to link (typically not used)
element.definition: CoverageToLink is typically not used in provider-initiated member match requests as providers are not linking historical coverage to new coverage. This element is only relevant for payer-to-payer member match operations.
